incidents.vector: Infostealer malware (Lumma) on a Context.ai employee device (seeded via Roblox game-cheat downloads) harvested OAuth tokens; attacker pivoted into a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account tied to Context.ai's AI Office Suite, then into Vercel environments and environment variables.
incidents.impact: A limited subset of Vercel customers had credentials and non-sensitive environment variables exposed; Vercel has forced credential rotations and engaged Mandiant/CrowdStrike, while a threat group identifying as ShinyHunters is reportedly auctioning stolen keys, source code, and databases for $2M. Vercel CEO assesses the attackers as AI-accelerated and highly sophisticated.

incidents.victim: Global ransomware / extortion victims (multi-ecosystem supply chain)
incidents.vector: Group calling itself TeamPCP used stolen developer credentials (publishing tokens, GitHub Actions secrets) to push credential-harvesting payloads into trusted repositories including LiteLLM (~97M monthly downloads), Checkmarx GitHub Actions, Aqua Security's Trivy, and a Telnyx PyPI package, then pivoted through the harvested cloud/API keys into additional targets.
incidents.impact: SANS ISC and Recorded Future tracked compromises cascading across five software distribution ecosystems in under a week, yielding access keys, cloud credentials, and AI API keys from thousands of downstream organizations; the group is threatening to leak 300+ GB of data and has been tied to Lapsus$-style extortion and at least one named ransomware victim.

incidents.victim: UK and allied government agencies; global Microsoft 365 users
incidents.vector: Russian GRU operators exploited known vulnerabilities in older Mikrotik and TP-Link SOHO routers to change DNS settings so that Microsoft Office OAuth authentication tokens transiting the routers were silently redirected to attacker-controlled servers, without deploying malware on endpoints.
incidents.impact: Peak activity in December 2025 ensnared more than 18,000 routers; Microsoft, Lumen's Black Lotus Labs, and NCSC-UK confirm more than 200 targeted organizations (including foreign ministries, law enforcement, and email providers) and 5,000 consumer devices caught up in the token-harvesting dragnet. FBI took down related infrastructure, but the UK NCSC warns state-backed intrusions now account for the majority of nationally significant cyberattacks on the UK.

incidents.victim: DigitalMint clients (nonprofit, hospitality, financial services, retail, medical)
incidents.vector: Angelo Martino, a ransomware negotiator at DigitalMint, secretly conspired with BlackCat (ALPHV) affiliates, leaking each victim's internal negotiation positions and insurance policy limits so co-conspirators could extract maximum ransoms from organizations Martino was supposed to be defending.
incidents.impact: DOJ says Martino helped extort roughly $75.3M across ten U.S. companies — including a ~$26.8M ransom from a nonprofit, ~$25.7M from a financial services firm, and ~$16.5M from a hospitality firm — before being fired and ultimately pleading guilty; $10M in assets, vehicles, a boat, and two Florida homes were seized from him.

incidents.victim: Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp and retail victims including Marks & Spencer
incidents.vector: Scattered Spider senior member Tyler 'Tylerb' Buchanan sent tens of thousands of SMS phishing lures in 2022 that tricked employees at major tech firms into surrendering SSO credentials, enabling help-desk social engineering intrusions and downstream SIM-swap attacks on crypto holders.
incidents.impact: Buchanan pleaded guilty in U.S. federal court to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft, admitting to stealing at least $8M in cryptocurrency from individual investors. He faces up to 22 years at his August 21 sentencing; the prosecution also connects the group to the 2025 M&S ransomware incident.

incidents.victim: Multiple organizations via compromised MSPs and software vendors
incidents.vector: Attackers exploited an unauthenticated RCE vulnerability (CVE-2026-1731) in BeyondTrust/Bomgar Remote Support and older Privileged Remote Access appliances, then used the compromised RMM to drop AnyDesk or Atera for persistence, add rogue accounts to Local/Domain Admin groups, and deploy LockBit 3.0 ransomware.
incidents.impact: Huntress tracked five incidents in two weeks: an April 3 dental-software vendor compromise cascaded to three downstream companies, an April 15 MSP compromise forced mass isolation of 78 downstream businesses with four confirmed onward compromises, and an April 12 attack ended in successful LockBit ransomware detonation.

incidents.victim: Global enterprises hiring remote IT workers
incidents.vector: DPRK-aligned 'Jasper Sleet' operators used generative AI to craft tailored personas matching real job postings, hit external Workday /hrrecruiting APIs to locate open technical roles, then interviewed and onboarded as remote IT workers using fabricated identities.
incidents.impact: Microsoft Threat Intelligence observed infiltrators accessing legitimate Workday profiles post-hire, setting up payroll accounts from DPRK-linked infrastructure, and then generating impossible-travel alerts in the first two months as they exfiltrated data from Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Exchange Online. Revenue funds DPRK weapons programs and in some cases enables data theft or extortion of the employer.

incidents.victim: ANTS — French National Agency for Secure Documents
incidents.vector: Unattributed intrusion into the ANTS web portal used to process applications for passports, national ID cards, residence permits, and driver's licenses; the French Interior Ministry has not disclosed the initial access method.
incidents.impact: Login credentials, names, email addresses, dates of birth, and unique account identifiers for an undisclosed number of individual and professional ANTS users may have been exposed; postal addresses, places of birth, and phone numbers are potentially affected as well, though uploaded document attachments were not breached. The incident follows recent cyber intrusions at the French Education Ministry and France's National Bank Accounts File.

incidents.victim: Kelp DAO / LayerZero / Aave and rsETH holders
incidents.vector: DPRK's TraderTraitor (Lazarus sub-group) compromised LayerZero's Decentralized Verifier Network infrastructure — reportedly via a malware-infected developer device — and, with Kelp's rsETH configured to rely on a single DVN, forged cross-chain messages to mint unbacked rsETH; simultaneous DDoS attacks against backup verifier systems prevented anomaly detection.
incidents.impact: Approximately $290-300M in crypto assets drained, the largest crypto heist of 2026 so far; Cyvers reports about $175M already being laundered through THORChain, Umbra, and BitTorrent mixers. Aave users faced liquidity freezes as they tried to withdraw against the fictitious collateral, and LayerZero says it will stop approving single-DVN configurations going forward.

incidents.vector: Attackers pre-positioned fake token pools on the Rhea Finance platform over several days, then abused a vulnerability in the protocol's margin-trading feature to route operations through the manipulated pools.
incidents.impact: Roughly $18.4M in assets stolen from the Rhea Finance DeFi platform; no attribution disclosed and recovery remains uncertain.

incidents.vector: Unspecified cyberattack on Friday April 17 prompted Tallahassee to take its IT network offline; surrounding Leon County disconnected its network from the city to prevent contamination, language that typically indicates a ransomware incident.
incidents.impact: Multiple city websites and public services remained unavailable into the weekend; the scope, data impact, and perpetrator have not been disclosed, and the county-level isolation suggests concern about lateral spread into regional government systems.

incidents.victim: Microsoft SharePoint and Windows customers worldwide
incidents.vector: Microsoft's April Patch Tuesday addressed 167 CVEs, including an actively exploited SharePoint Server spoofing zero-day (CVE-2026-32201) abused to impersonate trusted content in phishing and social-engineering campaigns, and the publicly disclosed Windows Defender 'BlueHammer' privilege-escalation flaw (CVE-2026-33825) for which exploit code was leaked pre-patch. Separately, CISA added seven CVEs to the KEV catalog including a Fortinet SQL-injection flaw (CVE-2026-21643) and an Adobe Acrobat prototype-pollution bug (CVE-2026-34621).
incidents.impact: Organizations running unpatched SharePoint faced in-the-wild spoofing attacks used to socially engineer employees, partners, and customers, while federal agencies were placed under BOD 22-01 remediation timelines for the new KEV entries; Google Chrome and Adobe Reader also shipped emergency fixes for additional zero-days exploited the same week.
